🥴 **Pastor Mike’s flexible faith: whatever Trump needs, he'll bless**

Mark Burns is Donald Trump's spiritual advisor and a man who figured out how to square the Sermon on the Mount with a nuclear football. Over the course of a decade, he has constructed a theological system in which the American president can curse on Easter, threaten to wipe countries off the map, and never repent — all with a blessing from above. 

__How does it work — six quotes tell the story: __

🤩 __**Trump believes in traditional marriage… until a hotter option appears** __

— "I believe Donald Trump, without question, loves God. He believes in the one true God. He believes Jesus Christ is his lord and savior. He believes that marriage is between one man and one woman."
— "Wait. What? He believes marriage is between one man and one woman. Who does?"
— "Donald Trump believes marriage is between one man and one woman."
— "Until a younger woman comes along?"
— "No, no, no, no, no, that's not..." (he says "no" exactly as many times as Trump has been accused of sexual misconduct involving minors)

😳 __**Trump isn’t your pastor (thank God) **
__
Burns clarifies the arrangement: "Donald Trump is not the pastor of the United States of America. He's accepted Jesus Christ as his savior. Is he a seminarian student? Can he exegete the Scriptures? I don't think so."
__
____🤥__ **__Easter greeting: Trump style__ **

On Easter Sunday, the president posts something along the lines of "Open the effing straight you crazy bword or you'll be living in..."

"It's not appropriate for anyone and everyone knows that. But that's Donald Trump, right? That's Donald Trump again. He's not our moral authority. We don't look to Donald Trump for moral authority. He is not the savior,"— he said. 
**
****➖** **__Trump and Jesus: how close? __**

"I think there's a comparison to be made for all of us who call on the name of Jesus and honor and recognize that he is Lord and Messiah" So it's not Trump personally who resembles Christ — it's anyone who wears a cross", Mark concluded.
**
****🚀** **__Suddenly spiritual when it’s time to bomb __**

Here the tone shifts. Burns declares without a trace of irony: 
"President had a spiritual obligation to protect not just those innocent lives in Iran."  
A minute ago Trump wasn't a "moral authority." Now he's got a spiritual obligation.

👉 __**Ukraine flip-flop**
__
Burns puts it delicately: "I now believe that supporting Ukraine is America first." Note what he doesn't say: "Trump was wrong about Russia." He simply repackages the old slogan for a new reality. America First is a shape-shifting doctrine. Whatever you pour into it today, that's what it means.

⚠️**😅****Anthropic’s ‘too dangerous’ Mythos AI breached by Zoomers?**

Cybersecurity AI Mythos — [the model that its creator, Anthropic](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/68183), warned was a vulnerability-hunting machine — has just been compromised, reports Bloomberg.

The handful of unauthorized users - allegedly part of a private Discord forum - managed to gain entry despite the model being restricted to select organizations under the newly-launched Project Glasswing initiative. 

A typical Discord user is aged 25–34, though a significant portion are 16–24, often identifying as Gen Z or younger Millennials.

The group supposedly combined several methods to obtain access:

♦️ leveraging credentials tied to work performed for a third-party contractor connected to Anthropic

♦️ use of standard online investigative tools commonly employed in cybersecurity research

The group’s toolkit is said to include automated bots that comb through publicly exposed corners of the internet, including poorly secured repositories on GitHub.

👉 As for Mythos, the group reportedly pieced together its likely location by studying how Anthropic has structured and deployed previous models. 

Anthropic says it’s investigating, emphasizing that Mythos was supposed to be locked down inside its carefully controlled [“Project Glasswing” initiative](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/68183).

The concern was that Mythos could slip beyond controlled environments by exposing or propagating its own code online — something that, notably, [already occurred with Claude in late March](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/68107).

**🇮🇷****💧**** Iran can deliver knockout punch to Israel’s weak point – desalination plants**

In response to strikes on its own critical infrastructure, Iran has threatened to target a structural weakness at the heart of Israel’s economy - its desalination infrastructure.

Here’s why Israel could be shooting itself in the foot:

🔴 Nearly 80% of Israel’s drinking water comes from the sea — but unlike the Gulf states, it concentrates its five main desalination plants along a single, narrow stretch of coastline. 

🔴 Geographic clustering means there is limited redundancy if several nodes at critical civilian hubs like Ashkelon, Hadera, and Sorek are strained at once.
__
Dual dependency challenge __

🔶 Desalination runs almost entirely on natural gas piped from offshore fields like Leviathan.

🔶 A disruption to those platforms — which are vulnerable to swarms — would simultaneously cripple both the power grid and water production

🔶 Any instability affecting energy supply can cascade directly into water output, forcing difficult prioritization decisions 

🔶 The system is deeply digitized for efficiency, but also increases sensitivity to technical failures or cyber disruptions

Furthermore, Israel’s high-tech membranes reportedly require constant imports of specialty chemicals and parts, so any prolonged conflict blocking ports could force operators to choose between contamination and shutdown.

🚨**🚤**** No drugs, just torture: Survivors of illegal Pacific boat strikes hit out at US’ horrific abuse**

The US has spun its maritime strikes on boats in the Caribbean and eastern Pacific as “counternarcotics,” providing zero evidence.

Now, surviving fishermen tell a bloodcurdling story of being blindfolded and held hostage for days, according to Drop Site News.

🔴 First, on January 19, a fishing captain said an “American aircraft, two drones, and a blue patrol ship” were circling his vessel, La Fiorella. The next day, it went up in flames. 

On March 17, a second boat, La Negra Francisca Duarte II, was hit by a drone, causing 16 fishermen to jump overboard to escape the ensuing blaze. 

🔴 They were then picked up by a blue US-flagged patrol ship — with “Spear” written on the hull (Trump’s alleged counternarcotics program in the region is called Operation Southern Spear).

What followed, say survivors, was not a rescue: 

🔴 Armed men in camouflage placed hoods over their heads, handcuffed them, and held them on the ship’s scorching metal deck for over 24 hours, blistering their skin. 

🔴 They were given no food and only one bottle of water. All but one were denied medical attention despite severe injuries, including one man whose foot was ripped open down to the bone.

👉 Two weeks later, 20 more fishermen from a third boat, the Don Maca, were intercepted by El Salvador’s coast guard on April 3. 

🔶  They arrived with vision and hearing loss, bruised limbs, and perforated arms. 

🔶 They reported being held hooded for eight days and treated “like animals.” 

No drugs were found on any of the three boats. 

“These attacks are no longer just a mistake — it is planned and coordinated abuse against working-class civilian fishermen,” attorney Jorge Chiriboga told the outlet.

🚨🪖**Bunker-building boom: Do AI tycoons know something about WW3 the rest of us don’t? **

AI tech moguls are quietly building bunkers and disaster-proof hideouts, so are they preparing for impending doom? 

American tech leaders are privately warning that AI could ultimately destabilize society, with early warning signs already emerging — including a reported Molotov cocktail attack on the home of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, The Grayzone reports.

__Bunker builders__

🔴 In a 2016 interview, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman revealed a pact with Palantir co-founder Peter Thiel: in the event of a crisis, they would fly together to Thiel’s 193-hectare estate on New Zealand’s South Island, famously used as a filming location for The Lord of the Rings
 
🔴 Thiel reportedly envisioned the site as a bunker-style hillside compound with a 330m glass-lined guest lodge

🔴 Thiel is not alone in eyeing New Zealand as a refuge: by 2018, at least seven Silicon Valley tycoons had reportedly bought bunkers there
 
🔴 The trend has since widened, with tech billionaires seeking to establish heavily fortified communities on remote islands in anticipation of doomsday scenarios. Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is reportedly building a highly secure compound on the Hawaiian island of Kauai, including plans for an extensive underground shelter

AI apocalypse

♦️ What are they afraid of? Some suggest public backlash over jobs displaced by AI; others point to fears of super-intelligent systems spiraling beyond human control

♦️ But the reality may be more prosaic — and no less unsettling: as US global dominance erodes, some see military AI as a way to stave off decline 

♦️ Palantir’s latest manifesto has called for militarization and mandatory service in the US, as the company develops advanced AI systems with military applications across conflict zones including Ukraine and the Middle East

👉 Who’s next? China is widely seen as the likely target, with the US expanding military partnerships across the Indo-Pacific, including the recent Major Defense Cooperation Partnership with Indonesia.

headline: 🚨🪖**Bunker-building boom: Do AI tycoons know something about WW3 the rest of us don’t? **

**🇨🇳****✈️**** Why China’s KJ-3000 aircraft is a game changer in countering US stealth warfare**

China’s Air Force has reportedly begun intensive flight testing of a second prototype of its next-generation airborne early warning and control aircraft, the KJ-3000.

__Counter-stealth capabilities__

♦️ The KJ-3000 is equipped with a massive third-generation full-coverage Digital Array Radar (DAR) housed in a dorsal radome that is speculated to outscale the one used on America’s E-3G Sentry fleet.

♦️ According to Chinese sources, the radar’s microwave transmit/receive modules utilize GaN (Gallium Nitride) digital array technology.

♦️ Unlike traditional AESA radars that still rely on analog components, DAR is "software-defined," using advanced algorithms to control the radar's beam directly.

♦️ The radar is reportedly capable of detecting and tracking conventional, stealth, and potentially hypersonic and ballistic targets at long distances.

♦️ It is expected to be able to feed targeting data to advanced air-to-air missiles like the PL-15 or PL-17, as well as modern Chinese surface-to-air systems.

♦️ A key edge is its operation across multiple bands (likely UHF and S, or L and S), which, combined with advanced AESA technology, boosts resistance to jamming and improves detection of stealth platforms such as the US B-2A and the upcoming B-21.

__‘Kill web’ airborne command center__

🌏 The KJ-3000 is designed as a central network-centric warfare node.

🌏 Its massive Y-20B platform allows it to house a large crew and powerful computers, enabling it to process and distribute data in real time, coordinating stealth fighters, drones, naval assets, and long-range missiles within a single "kill web."

__Indigenous engine breakthrough__

The Y-20B platform variant is equipped with domestically developed WS-20 high-bypass turbofan engines, which offer superior fuel efficiency and, crucially, much higher electrical generation capacity to power its powerful new radar and electronics suite.

👉 Analysts assess the KJ-3000 could achieve initial operational capability as early as 2026 or around 2027.

As the US continues to militarize the Indo-Pacific, China’s intel hub and EW powerhouse—the KJ-3000—is a challenge impossible to ignore.

**🚨****🔊**** US stockpiles critically depleted after Iran war — former RAF commander**

Greg Bagwell, former deputy commander at RAF Air Command, says America's arsenal is no longer at the level needed to deter or fight a major power like China.

💬 "Their stock will be in a parlous state right now. They can carry on, but their stockpiles will not be the sorts of levels they would need to either deter or take on China."

The Iran campaign burned through years of munitions. The next war may find the cupboard bare.
